finding: pan-reset effect only zeroes translate when mode==='fit'; zooming OUT while panned in actual mode never re-clamps translate to the smaller overflow, leaving the image offset off-screen until the user drags again

active-highlight effect deps [supported,activeIndex,count] omit rangesRef identity; a MutationObserver rebuild with same count/index but fresh Ranges leaves HL_ACTIVE stale/detached (active highlight vanishes, no re-scroll)

Ctrl/Cmd-F onKeyDown on a tabIndex=-1 div only fires when a descendant is focused; text/markdown content has no focusable elements so activeElement stays body and native find opens instead — the keyboard-open path is effectively dead
container cursor derives from drag.current (a ref) at render, but onPointerDown mutates the ref without a re-render, so the cursor never switches to 'grabbing' during a pan
zoomed/actual image container is overflow-hidden and pannable only by pointer-drag; no role/tabIndex/keydown, so keyboard-only users cannot pan off-screen regions of a zoomed image
closing the find bar unmounts the focused input without restoring focus to the FindButton/region; keyboard focus drops to document.body

CSS.highlights registry names 'file-find'/'file-find-active' are process-global; two concurrently-mounted FindableRegions clobber each other's highlights and either unmount's clearHighlights deletes the other's
CopySelectionButton uses global window.getSelection() with no check the selection lies inside the viewer, contradicting its own doc comment — it copies any selection anywhere on the page

Segmented onChange calls the action via the render proxy (Stores.File.setImageViewMode) while its sibling zoom buttons use Stores.File.__state; per the CopyButton rationale a handler-context call should use __state
rebuild() re-walks the whole subtree + rebuilds every Range on each query change with no debounce (O(N) per keystroke)
the MutationObserver setup effect depends on rebuild whose identity changes per query, so the observer is disconnected+reconnected (and rebuild re-run) on every keystroke
onPointerMove calls setTranslate every event (a re-render) and overflow() reads clientWidth/naturalWidth each move (forced reflow), producing a render+reflow storm while panning
FindButton tooltip hardcodes 'Find (Ctrl+F)' though the handler also accepts Cmd on macOS, so Mac users see the wrong modifier
the spec claims ITEM-3 (pan) coverage but never performs a drag, so the real pan path (pointer capture, overflow geometry, clampTranslate integration) is exercised only by pure-math unit tests
the TreeWalker text collection + global-offset→{node,offset} Range mapping (incl. cross-text-node matches) has no unit test; e2e only covers single-text-node matches
the first unit test asserts only typeof===boolean and passes regardless of correctness; the supported=true branch is never positively exercised

reviewed: ApiClient.File.get rejection maps to the not-found state, cancelled-flag guards the async setState on unmount, and copy/clipboard failures are caught with a message — no unhandled-rejection or missing error branch found

reviewed: iframe sandbox='' unchanged; new-tab uses download-with-token (server forces Content-Disposition: attachment, no inline script exec) with noopener,noreferrer; the injected <style> color-mix content is fully static — no XSS/exfil surface introduced
reviewed: /files/:fileId route is requiresAuth; the fetch is owner-scoped server-side so a foreign UUID yields 404→not-found (no cross-user leak); no client-side authz bypass

reviewed: ApiClient.File.get returns the FileEntity directly (matches getFileEntityById usage); the route object shape (path/element/requiresAuth/layout) matches chat/projects; openFileInNewTab/generateDownloadToken consumed as declared
